premise: After my husband died, his adult daughter from his first marriage handed me a forged lease and evicted me from the apartment above the bakery we’d built together, pocketing $75,000 of his life insurance she claimed I was never entitled to. I let her believe she’d won for two years, then revealed that I’d bought the entire building years earlier with a trust my grandmother left me — I evicted her bakery, reopened my own café across the street, and sent her a bill for market-rate back rent that forced her into bankruptcy.
